title: jina-ai/reader — URL to LLM-friendly markdown (r.jina.ai)
summary: Production TypeScript codebase behind r.jina.ai — converts any URL to LLM-friendly markdown via curl-impersonate + Puppeteer + Readability + Turndown, deployed as 6 Cloud Run services from one Docker image. Strong patterns (tiered fallback chain, multi-provider SERP generator, LD_PRELOAD TLS fingerprint) undermined by 3 S0 security issues, zero automated tests, and a private submodule that blocks self-hosting. Best treated as a hosted service to call, not a codebase to adopt.
creator_view: See creator-view.md. Key insight: this repo's knowledge is not the stack — it's curl-impersonate as the default fetcher with Puppeteer as escape hatch, plus one-image-six-services Cloud Run deployment via --args. For SoNash, the highest-leverage move is to decide whether r.jina.ai becomes a fetch backend for /website-analysis (instrument superpowers-chrome gaps first, then decide). Port patterns selectively; avoid adopting the codebase.

candidates.name: Multi-provider fallback generator (iterProviders)
candidates.description: Generator yields provider clients in preference order (Jina internal -> Serper Google -> Serper Bing). for...of loop catches per-provider errors and advances. First success wins. Query-shortening fallback strips last N/4 terms up to 4 iterations, RTL-aware.

candidates.name: x-* request header protocol
candidates.description: 11+ x-* headers control runtime fetch behavior (x-target-selector, x-wait-for-selector, x-timeout, x-cache-tolerance, x-no-cache, x-respond-with, x-proxy-url, x-set-cookie, x-with-generated-alt, x-engine, x-return-format). Clean separation of URL identity from fetch configuration.

candidates.name: Tiered fetch fallback chain with sideLoad body hint
candidates.description: cachedScrap() executes: Firestore cache -> curl-impersonate fetch -> (if thin <42 tokens or non-200) proxy retry -> Puppeteer with body passed as sideLoad hint to avoid double-fetch -> jsdom narrowing via Readability -> Turndown format -> stale cache fallback on any throw.

candidates.name: curl-impersonate as Docker LD_PRELOAD base layer
candidates.description: Two-stage Dockerfile: FROM lwthiker/curl-impersonate:0.6-chrome-slim-bullseye -> FROM node:22. Copy libcurl-impersonate.so from stage 1, set LD_PRELOAD + CURL_IMPERSONATE=chrome116. Every outbound libcurl hop impersonates Chrome 116 TLS fingerprint.

candidates.name: Multi-target deployment via --args override
candidates.description: One Docker image deployed 6 times to Cloud Run by varying --args build/stand-alone/<entry>.js at deploy time. Inside container, tsyringe DI resolves the correct API host class (CrawlerHost / SearcherHost / SerpHost) into the same Koa shell. Zero code duplication.

candidates.name: Readability + Turndown + Puppeteer extraction pipeline
candidates.description: Reference stack: Puppeteer renders -> linkedom parses -> @mozilla/readability narrows to article body -> Turndown (with GFM plugin) emits markdown. All stages swappable via DI. Optional VLM alt-text via OpenAI.

candidates.name: r.jina.ai hosted service as fetch backend
candidates.description: The running hosted service at https://r.jina.ai/<url> handles SPA, PDFs, JS-rendered pages, cookies, proxies. Free tier + paid. Viable as a fallback fetch backend for /website-analysis in CAS. Adoption cost: one HTTP call. Trade-off: vendor lock-in on a core CAS primitive.

candidates.name: curl-impersonate + TLS fingerprint concept
candidates.description: Anti-bot defenses fingerprint the full TLS/TCP stack, not just User-Agent. Real Chrome vs headless Chrome differ at the cipher suite level. curl-impersonate rewrites libcurl to emit Chrome 116's exact fingerprint. Understanding this explains why firecrawl/crawl4ai leak through some sites.

candidates.name: Commercial OSS without tests as an architectural stance
candidates.description: Reader runs at commercial scale with zero automated tests. Quality floor is TypeScript strict + lint + live observability + traffic volume. Works for the originator (Jina has Cloud Logging + dashboards + traffic); transfers poorly to adopters. File as a cautionary stance, not an emulation target.

candidates.name: Blog: Reader for search-grounding to improve factuality of LLMs
candidates.description: Jina blog post explaining the rationale for s.jina.ai: LLMs hallucinate less when grounded on actual fetched web content. Search-then-read pattern vs search-only. Thesis alignment with SoNash's deep-research evidence-gathering philosophy.

candidates.name: Colab notebook: full-site crawling methodology
candidates.description: The only practical multi-URL example using r.jina.ai. Demonstrates URL enumeration, rate limiting, result merging. Useful reference IF SoNash adds a bulk analyze mode to /website-analysis.

candidates.name: DeepWiki-as-architecture-docs
candidates.description: README 'How it works' section is literally an image badge linking to deepwiki.com. Zero first-party architecture exposition in a 10.5k-star OSS repo. Do not emulate — own the architecture story in the repo, don't outsource to auto-wiki.

candidates.name: Public repo + private submodule requirement
candidates.description: Public repo imports from SSH-only private thinapps-shared submodule containing rate-limit, secrets, DAOs, decorators. README dismisses it as 'not integral' but the code does not compile without it. 'Open source' in license only, not in self-host sense. Erodes adopter trust.

candidates.name: Zero tests in a 10k-star production service
candidates.description: No .test.ts, no .spec.ts, no describe(). firebase-functions-test is devDep but never imported. CI is deploy-only behind L4 health check. The stance requires compensating observability that adopters don't inherit. Cautionary: 'no tests' works for originators, not forks.

adoption_verdict: Self-host: AVOID (3 S0s + private submodule blocks compile + zero tests). Hosted service use: TRIAL (instrument current /website-analysis fetch gaps before committing). Creator lens: EXTRACT selected patterns (iterProviders generator, x-* headers, TLS fingerprint Docker layer).
